title: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie

seo.title: Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma Recipe - Authentic & Easy
seo.metaDescription: Master authentic Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma at home! This easy recipe brings Middle Eastern flavors to your kitchen with juicy, spiced chicken. Perfect for a crowd!
longTailKeywords: how to make Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma
authentic Middle Eastern Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A whole Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma, beautifully spiced and golden brown, ready to be carved.
seo.imageCaption: Experience the rich, aromatic flavors of the Middle East with this homemade Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma, perfect for a family meal.

description: Transport your taste buds to the heart of the Middle East with this succulent Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma. A whole chicken is bathed in a rich, aromatic spice blend and slow-roasted to perfection on a rotisserie, ensuring crispy skin and incredibly juicy meat. Serve this flavorful dish in warm pita bread with fresh toppings for an unforgettable meal.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Ensure your whole chicken is thoroughly cleaned and patted completely dry with paper towels. This helps the marinade adhere better and promotes crispy skin. Remove any giblets from the cavity.

instructions.title: Mix the Shawarma Marinade
instructions.text: In a medium bowl, combine the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, ground cumin, ground coriander, smoked paprika, ground turmeric, ground cardamom, ground cinnamon, ground allspice, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until a fragrant paste forms.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Generously rub the shawarma marinade all over the chicken, making sure to get it into the crevices and under the skin if possible. For best results, place the chicken in a large Ziploc bag or covered dish and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, allowing the flavors to deeply penetrate.

instructions.title: Set Up the Rotisserie
instructions.text: Preheat your grill or oven to medium-high heat (around 375-400F or 190-200C) if using a rotisserie attachment. Secure the marinated chicken onto the rotisserie spit, ensuring it is balanced and trussed properly so that no parts hang loosely and interfere with rotation.

instructions.title: Rotisserie Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the rotisserie spit with the chicken onto your grill or into the oven. Cook for approximately 1 hour 30 minutes to 1 hour 45 minutes, or until the internal temperature of the thickest part of the thigh reaches 165F (74C). The chicken should be beautifully golden brown and crispy.

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, carefully remove the chicken from the rotisserie spit. Transfer it to a cutting board and tent loosely with foil. Let the chicken rest for 10-15 minutes before carving. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring a tender and moist result. Carve and serve hot with your favorite shawarma accompaniments.

tips: To achieve truly authentic flavor, consider adding a pinch of ground cloves or a small amount of grated fresh ginger to your shawarma marinade.
For extra tender chicken, you can add 2 tablespoons of plain yogurt or buttermilk to the marinade. The lactic acid helps to tenderize the meat.
Serve your Rotisserie Chicken Shawarma carved into thin slices, wrapped in warm pita bread with a drizzle of tahini sauce, chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, and pickled turnips for a complete experience.
article: Chicken Shawarma Spiced Rotisserie is a beloved Middle Eastern Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
